etymology.description: semantic 言(言) (yán, "speech") + phonetic 𢆶(𢆶) (biàn) + 夂 radical (walk; movement) -> change or transformation
etymology.interpretation: The character combines 言(言) (yán, "speech"), suggesting communication, laws, or commands that direct change, with 𢆶(𢆶), providing the phonetic component for the sound 'biàn'. The 夂 radical (walk; movement) signifies action or the process of transformation. Together, they convey an idea of change or transformation initiated or described through spoken words or commands.
etymology.simplification: The traditional 變 was simplified by reducing its components to 辶 (movement) + 又 (again) in 变, keeping the core idea of change or transformation.

examples.chinese: 天气开始变暖(天氣開始變暖)。
examples.english: The weather is starting to warm up.

examples.chinese: 他变得越来越自信(他變得越來越自信)。
examples.pinyin: Tā biàn de yuè lái yuè zìxìn.
examples.english: He is becoming more and more confident.

examples.chinese: 她决定改变生活方式(她決定改變生活方式)。
examples.english: She decided to change her lifestyle.
